headline: Marine Le Pen decides to run for president after appeals convition
description: The far-right leader said she would appeal to France's highest court after her embezzlement conviction was upheld but her ban on holding elected office was shortened, allowing her to stand in the 2027 election.

headline: US launches new strikes on Iran after Hormuz attacks
On the first day of the NATO summit in Ankara, Rutte unveils a wave of defense contracts worth billions of euros
Two Israeli NGOs warn of a 'de facto annexation at an unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
description: Israel now controls 18% of the occupied territories, compared to 7% before the war in Gaza. A report from the non-profit organizations Peace Now and Kerem Navot has been documenting the acceleration of land grabs, the construction of settler-only roads and the expulsion of local communities over the past three and a half years.

headline: In Quebec, 'moving day' overshadowed by housing crisis
description: A unique tradition in the Canadian province, tens of thousands of people move on the same day, July 1. But in recent years, this ritual has been marred by serious difficulties in finding new housing.

headline: 2026 World Cup: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to reach quarter-finals
description: Switzerland beat Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a goalless draw on Tuesday. The Swiss will face Argentina in Kansas City on Saturday after Lionel Messi's team earlier beat Egypt 3-2 in a wild game in Atlanta.

headline: Book reveals Trump's improvisation and concentration of power
description: Two journalists from the 'New York Times' retrace the early months of the US president's second term, highlighting the ruptures and upheavals his approach to power has unleashed.

PFAS: Less than 2% of emissions could be removed from environment, even with massive investments
description: A study by scientists and journalists from the Forever Pollution Project, coordinated by Le Monde, reveals that cleanup efforts are largely ineffective and highlights the urgent need for strict limits on emissions.

headline: One hundred early works by Claude Monet reunited at Le Havre's André Malraux Museum
description: The MuMa exhibition, showcasing drawings, caricatures and paintings, places the painter in his family and regional context.

headline: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
description: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.
